paper_title: Measurement of fracture toughness of an ice core from Antarctica

methods.description: This method describes the process of measuring the Mode I fracture toughness of an ice core sample from Antarctica. Fracture toughness is a material parameter that characterizes a material's resistance to crack growth, which is crucial for predicting material failure under stress. The method involves applying a load to the sample until fracture occurs and using the maximum load, sample geometry, and loading roller positioning to calculate the critical fracture toughness.

steps.description: Apply an external load perpendicular to the fracture surface of the ice core sample until the point of fracture is reached.
steps.searchable_summary: apply load to ice core sample until fracture

steps.description: Measure the maximum load prior to failure, P max.
Calculate the critical fracture toughness using the maximum measured force, the sample geometry, and the positioning of the loading rollers.
